title: Brex Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Brex** is a corporate-card + spend-management platform exposing a REST API at `https://platform.brexapis.com`. This connector â€” `BrexConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "brex"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) â€” wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Brex:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/v2/users` | List + read users, current user lookup |
| Cards | `/v2/cards` | List + read corporate cards |
| Card transactions | `/v2/transactions/card/primary` | List + read card transactions |
| Expenses | `/v1/expenses/card` | List + read expense records |
| Departments | `/v2/departments` | Org-tree departments |
| Locations | `/v2/locations` | Office locations |
| Vendors | `/v1/vendors` | AP vendors |
| Receipts | `/v1/expenses/card/receipt_match` | Receipts attached to expenses |
| Budgets | `/v2/budgets` | Budgets |
| Spend Limits | `/v2/spend_limits` | Spend limits per program |

The connector normalises transactions + expenses + users into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC â€” all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::BrexHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Brex access token

1. Sign in to https://dashboard.brex.com.
2. Open **Developer â†’ Tokens**.
3. Either:
   - Click **Create new token** and grant the scopes you need (Cards Read, Transactions Read, Expenses Read+Write, Users Read, etc.). Brex shows the token only once â€” copy it immediately.
   - Or set up an OAuth2 client-credentials app and mint a token via `POST /oauth2/default/v1/token`.
4. The token value is what you paste into the `access_token` install field.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP â†’ Manage Connectors â†’ Brex**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `access_token` (secret) | Token from step 3 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://platform.brexapis.com` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `access_token` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `BrexHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /v2/users/me` as a lightweight check. On 2xx â†’ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 â†’ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 5xx / network â†’ `OFFLINE + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Brex REST API uses **Bearer-token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). Tokens can be created two ways:

1. **Personal access tokens** â€” generated in the Brex Dashboard â†’ Developer â†’ Tokens. Long-lived; revoked manually.
2. **OAuth2 client credentials** â€” mint short-lived tokens via `POST /oauth2/default/v1/token`. The connector treats the resulting access_token the same as a personal token.

### Credential model

- `access_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <token>`.
- No `X-Brex-*` headers are required.
- No tenant-of-tenant scoping (Brex tokens are account-scoped).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `access_token`. Initialises `BrexHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Brex endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a surface-compat `TokenInfo` carrying the configured access_token â€” no exchange happens. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /v2/users/me` â€” minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called â€” there is no refresh path for personal tokens; OAuth2 short-lived tokens are refreshed externally. |
| token storage | None (the connector uses the stored config value). |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` â€” never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `access_token` | secret | yes | `self.access_token` | Authorization header value (Bearer) |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://platform.brexapis.com`) | Override for Brex sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["access_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_BREX_BASE = "https://platform.brexapis.com"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `BrexConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::BrexHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `access_token`; initialise `BrexHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth â€” returns a TokenInfo carrying the configured access_token.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /v2/users/me`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Brex card transactions + expenses + users â†’ `NormalizedDocument` â†’ `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async get_current_user() -> Dict`
`GET /v2/users/me`.

### `async list_users(*, cursor=None, limit=50, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/users?limit={N}&cursor={C}[&status={S}]`.

### `async get_user(user_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/users/{user_id}`.

children.title: Cards
children.content:
### `async list_cards(*, cursor=None, limit=50, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/cards?limit={N}&cursor={C}[&user_id={U}]`.

### `async get_card(card_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/cards/{card_id}`.

children.title: Transactions
children.content:
### `async list_transactions(*, cursor=None, limit=100, posted_at_start=None, expand=None) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/transactions/card/primary` â€” list card transactions, cursor-paginated.

### `async get_transaction(transaction_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/transactions/card/primary/{transaction_id}`.

children.title: Expenses + Receipts
children.content:
### `async list_expenses(*, cursor=None, limit=50, expense_type=None, status=None, payment_status=None, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/expenses/card` â€” filter by `expense_type[]`, `status[]`, `payment_status[]`, `user_id[]`.

### `async get_expense(expense_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/expenses/card/{expense_id}`.

### `async list_receipts(*, cursor=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/expenses/card/receipt_match`.

children.title: Departments Â· Locations Â· Vendors
children.content:
### `async list_departments(*, cursor=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/departments`.

### `async list_locations(*, cursor=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/locations`.

### `async list_vendors(*, cursor=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/vendors`.

children.title: Budgets + Spend Limits
children.content:
### `async list_budgets(*, cursor=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/budgets`.

### `async list_spend_limits(*, cursor=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/spend_limits`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
BrexError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
â”śâ”€â”€ BrexAuthError                      # 401 / 403
â”śâ”€â”€ BrexBadRequestError                # 400
â”śâ”€â”€ BrexNotFoundError                  # 404
â”śâ”€â”€ BrexConflictError                  # 409
â”śâ”€â”€ BrexRateLimitError                 # 429 â€” retry_after_s
â””â”€â”€ BrexServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
BrexNetworkError = BrexServerError      # legacy name
BrexNotFound     = BrexNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `BrexNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 â†’ ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)   â€” logged as warning
network â†’ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `access_token` blank | Re-enter the token in ACP. Brex shows it only once at creation â€” rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Brex â€” token lacks scope | In Brex Dashboard â†’ Developer â†’ Tokens, edit the token scopes (Users, Cards, Transactions, Expenses Read at minimum). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 â€” token revoked or expired (OAuth2 short-lived) | Rotate the personal token, or mint a new OAuth2 access_token via `POST /oauth2/default/v1/token` and update the install field. |
| `brex.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Brex per-token rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_user(user_id)` | User does not belong to this Brex account | Verify the `user_id` and the token's account scope. |
| 404 on `get_card(card_id)` | Wrong card_id or card terminated | List cards first via `list_cards()` to discover valid IDs. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'BrexNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed â€” `BrexNetworkError = BrexServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Cursor pagination loops forever | Brex returned a malformed `next_cursor` | The sync loop exits when `next_cursor` is falsy â€” verify the API response shape; open a Brex support ticket if needed. |
